text.title: batch-cache-stack Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for batch-cache-stack as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Embedding the timestamp, request ID, or row index inside the cached system prompt because it "feels safer."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every item's prefix differs by 8 bytes, so every item is a cache miss; you pay 1.25× cache-write per item AND get the 50% batch discount only — net cost is HIGHER than uncached batch.

failure-mode.description: Putting the per-item context (URL, doc text, user question) at the START of the messages section instead of as a regular user turn at the end.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cache hits require the variable content to come AFTER the cache_control breakpoint; fronting it makes every item miss — same failure mode as drift, harder to spot.
